methodsAttempted.query: site:sheffield.ac.uk University of Sheffield generative AI policy students assessment academic integrity AI tools
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official StudySkills academic-integrity, education-principles, assessment, PGR, assistive-technology, critical-use, hub, and essay-report pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:sheffield.ac.uk University of Sheffield generative AI guidance staff teaching assessment artificial intelligence policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official education principles, assessment guidance, Academic Skills, IT Services productivity principles, PGR guidance, StudySkills hub, academic-integrity guidance, and Library copyright guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:sheffield.ac.uk University of Sheffield GenAI privacy data protection approved tools Copilot ChatGPT artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found PGR privacy guidance, IT Services approved-tool and data-protection guidance, education principles, student academic-integrity guidance, assessment guidance, and non-policy/news/PDF results that were not used as evidence.

Followed official links from verified pages to Library copyright guidance and to staff/student pages that redirected to University CAS login. Login-gated staff/student pages were rejected without bypass attempts.

artifacts.summary: Official Sheffield discovery located public University-controlled evidence for GenAI education principles, student academic integrity, assessment disclosure, PGR research use, IT/productivity data protection, approved-tool guidance, and Library copyright guidance. Login-gated staff/student pages were marked inaccessible and were not used as evidence. QS 2026 target metadata was confirmed separately as rank 92, rowNumber 92, The University of Sheffield, United Kingdom; that ranking metadata was not used as policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after collecting public official University of Sheffield sources with AI-specific guidance sufficient for staged claim/evidence candidates.
Do not access staff-only, student-only, CAS login, Google Docs, MFA, CAPTCHA, or other restricted targets.
Do not use search snippets, descriptions, third-party summaries, or QS ranking metadata as policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Sheffield says every undergraduate programme integrates GenAI literacy through structured teaching activities and formal assessment, while giving staff and students clarity about acceptable AI use across assessments.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Sheffield identifies Google Gemini as the institutionally supported GenAI tool for learning and teaching, and says Gemini should be used where possible to support those activities.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For student-facing tools outside Google Suite, the University says the New IT Solution Request Process is followed to check data-protection and information-security compliance before those tools are made available on a use-case basis.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: The University of Sheffield staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for The University of Sheffield uses public official University-controlled sources only. Approved staged claims cover education GenAI principles, Google Gemini support and review of non-Google tools, student academic-integrity and disclosure guidance, assessment disclosure templates, PGR research and thesis guidance, staff productivity/approved-tool/data-protection principles, and Library copyright guidance. Login-gated staff/student pages were marked inaccessible and were not used as evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
QS 2026 ranking metadata was used only to confirm the crawl target row and was not used as policy evidence.
Staff and student hub pages that redirected to University CAS login were not accessed, bypassed, or used as evidence.
Public Google Docs linked from Sheffield pages were not used as source evidence because the staged bundle is limited to University-controlled public pages.
Claims from PGR guidance are scoped to postgraduate researchers and thesis/research contexts, not to every student assessment.
Claims from IT Services productivity principles are scoped to administration, professional services, research support, and teaching-related support tasks.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
